segments.text: "Hear this word, O house of Israel, this lamentation I take up against you:
Fallen is Virgin Israel, never to rise again. She lies abandoned on her land, with no one to raise her up.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "This is what the Lord GOD says:
The city that marches out a thousand strongwill only see a hundred return, and the one that marches out a hundred strongwill have but ten left in the house of Israel.
segments.speaker: the Lord God

segments.text: "For this is what the LORD says to the house of Israel:
Seek Me and live!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Do not seek Bethel or go to Gilgal; do not journey to Beersheba, for Gilgal will surely go into exile, and Bethel will come to nothing.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Seek the LORD and live, or He will sweep like fire through the house of Joseph; it will devour everything, with no one at Bethel to extinguish it.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: There are those who turn justice into wormwood and cast righteousness to the ground.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
He who made the Pleiades and Orion, who turns darkness into dawn
and darkens day into night, who summons the waters of the sea and pours them over the face of the earth—
the LORD is His name—
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: He flashes destruction on the strong, so that fury comes upon the stronghold.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: There are those who hate the one who reproves in the gate and despise him who speaks with integrity.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore, because you trample on the poor and exact from him a tax of grain, you will never livein the stone houses you have built; you will never drink the winefrom the lush vineyards you have planted.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For I know that your transgressions are many and your sins are numerous. You oppress the righteous by taking bribes; you deprive the poor of justice in the gate.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore, the prudent keep silent in such times, for the days are evil.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Seek good, not evil, so that you may live. And the LORD, the God of Hosts, will be with you, as you have claimed.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Hate evil and love good; establish justice in the gate. Perhaps the LORD, the God of Hosts, will be gracious to the remnant of Joseph.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: "Therefore this is what the LORD, the God of Hosts, the Lord, says:
There will be wailing in all the public squares and cries of ‘Alas! Alas!’ in all the streets. The farmer will be summoned to mourn, and the mourners to wail.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: There will be wailing in all the vineyards, for I will pass through your midst,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: says the LORD.

"Woe to you who long for the Day of the LORD! What will the Day of the LORD be for you?
It will be darkness and not light.
"It will be like a man who flees from a lion, only to encounter a bear, or who enters his house and rests his hand against the wall, only to be bitten by a snake.

"Will not the Day of the LORDbe darkness and not light,
even gloom with no brightness in it?
I hate, I despise your feasts! I cannot st and the stench of your solemn assemblies.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Even though you offer Me burnt offerings and grain offerings, I will not accept them; for your peace offerings of fattened cattleI will have no regard.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Take away from Me the noise of your songs! I will not listen to the music of your harps.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But let justice roll on like a river, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Did you bring Me sacrifices and offeringsforty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
You have taken along Sakkuth your king and Kaiwan your star god,
the idols you made for yourselves.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore I will send you into exile beyond Damascus,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says the LORD, whose name is the God of Hosts.
